top_signals.title: China is building launch pads near its nuclear missile silos
top_signals.summary: China’s nuclear missiles can reach any U.S. city. Now, satellite images show Beijing is building a sprawling web of military sites near its nuclear silos.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: At French Army FPV competition, NATO soldiers race drones and swap tips
top_signals.summary: “When I put on the mask, I no longer exist in this reality,” Chief Cpl. Clément said after completing the course.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Atlantic Union Bank and former employee of Frost Bank
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Atlantic Union Bank and former employee of Frost Bank
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve Board requests public comment on a proposal to establish a "payment account," which legally eligible financial institutions could use for the specific purpose of clearing and settling their payments

top_signals.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
top_signals.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Gap CEO Remains Bullish Despite Lowering Sales Outlook
top_signals.summary:
Gap Inc. CEO Richard Dickson says the retailer delivered progress in the quarter, even after lowering its sales outlook. He says three out of their four brands are growing and they're raising the earnings outlook. Dickson speaks to Bloomberg's Lisa Abramowicz.

 (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin ETFs bleed $2.8B in record nine-day outflow streak
top_signals.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded a nine-day outflow streak totaling $2.84 billion, surpassing an eight-session outflow run in February 2025.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Modernising money and markets − speech by Sarah Breeden
Daniel Boulud's COVID-Era Bet
top_signals.summary: Five years after opening Le Pavillon during the height of COVID, Michelin-starred chef and Dinex Group Owner Daniel Boulud and SL Green CEO Marc Holliday joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about how luxury dining helped reinvent New York office real estate. They discuss the return-to-office boom, hospitality-driven workplaces, and why restaurants have become a key tool for attracting talent and premium tenants. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Sakanaction’s overseas breakthrough came from a viral dance meme
top_signals.summary: A video of an Indonesian preteen dancing on a boat became the unlikely springboard for Japanese rockers Sakanaction to gain global attention.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Prices for over 1,000 food items in Japan set to rise in June
top_signals.summary: Seasoning accounted for the largest share of products set to see higher prices, followed by processed food.
